lesson.menu_desc: Look back at blocks one to three and set goals for the second half. Talk about how you have changed with used to + base verb (I used to be nervous on calls, but now I am confident; we used to lose deals on price) -- past habits and states that are no longer true -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 19 -- Negotiation Recap & Mid-program Review
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Roberto Rezende | Aula 19 -- Negotiation Recap & Mid-program Review

listenings.text: Hi Roberto, this is Amy. We are at the halfway point of your program, so I want to share a quick mid-program review with you. Honestly, the progress has been a real milestone. Let me reflect on where you started. You used to be very nervous before client calls, and you used to read every line from a script. Now you lead the call with confidence and you handle questions in the moment. On the numbers side, the team used to lose deals on price alone, because we did not know how to defend value. Now you assess each client, you negotiate the terms, and you close the deal with a clean follow-up. There have been a few setbacks, of course. The Shanghai delay was one, and the difficult client in March was another. But every setback helped us refine the approach. For the second half, here is what I want you to focus on. First, keep the momentum on the bigger accounts. Second, consolidate everything you have learned about negotiation into one clear style that is yours. And third, set one ambitious goal, like leading a full client presentation in English on your own. You have come a long way. Let us assess the outcome together and plan the next steps. Talk soon.

This is a short voice note reflecting on my own progress at the mid-point of the program. When I think back, the change is a real milestone for me. I used to avoid speaking English in meetings, and I used to let a colleague answer for me. Now I open the meeting, I present the numbers, and I answer the hard questions myself. I used to panic when a client pushed back on price, but now I stay calm, I assess the situation, and I negotiate the terms. There have been setbacks, like the deal we nearly lost in March, but each one helped me refine how I handle pressure. For the second half, I want to keep the momentum and consolidate my new negotiation style. My main goal is to lead a full client presentation in English without help, and to close a big account on my own. I used to think fluent business English was years away. Now I can assess the outcome of my own work and see how far I have come. I am ready to set the next goal and reflect on it again at the end of the program.

extra_audio.text: First, look back at where you started at the beginning of the program. Then, reflect on what used to be hard and how you have improved. After that, name one real setback and what you learned from it. Next, set one clear, ambitious goal for the second half. Finally, decide how you will keep the momentum and consolidate your progress.
